issues.summary: Improve Apple Pay Support when there are multiple processors of the same currency
issues.description:
Our current logic for Apple Pay matches the payment type of Apple Pay to the the processor that has a matching CurrencyCode. The concern is that the some Currency Codes have multiple processors so it could choose the wrong one.

Example:

- CurrencyCode: EUR

- Processor: GNEU-EUR

- Processor: GP-EUR

Expectation:

- For an Apple Pay transaction, we should choose the processor that matches the facility of the tee time

- Scenario:

- FID - 19631

- Book an Apple Pay tee time

- Use the GNEU-EUR processor since that is what is configured at the facility

- Scenario:

- FID - 1951

- Book an Apple Pay tee times

- Use the GP-EUR processor since that is what is configured at the facility

Testing:

- Need to do full regression of Apple Pay

- Hot Deals

- $0 due online course time with payfac for reservations

- $0 due online course time without payfac for reservations

- $ due online course time with payfac for reservations

- $ due online course time without payfac for reservations

- Prepaid course times with and without payfac

- GolfPass Paid

- GolfPass Free

- Renewals

- No Shows

- Find an Apple Pay transaction with one of the old ChargeBys for Apple Pay → Make sure you can refund that after the data cleanup script is run

- Work with Yoel to set up test data

- Do a no show with and old ChargeBy as well
issues.descriptionHtml:
Our current logic for Apple Pay matches the payment type of Apple Pay to the the processor that has a matching CurrencyCode. The concern is that the some Currency Codes have multiple processors so it could choose the wrong one.

Example:

  • CurrencyCode: EUR

  • Processor: GNEU-EUR

  • Processor: GP-EUR

Expectation:

  • For an Apple Pay transaction, we should choose the processor that matches the facility of the tee time

  • Scenario:

    • FID - 19631

    • Book an Apple Pay tee time

    • Use the GNEU-EUR processor since that is what is configured at the facility

  • Scenario:

    • FID - 1951

    • Book an Apple Pay tee times

    • Use the GP-EUR processor since that is what is configured at the facility

Testing:

  • Need to do full regression of Apple Pay

    • Hot Deals

    • $0 due online course time with payfac for reservations

    • $0 due online course time without payfac for reservations

    • $ due online course time with payfac for reservations

    • $ due online course time without payfac for reservations

    • Prepaid course times with and without payfac

    • GolfPass Paid

    • GolfPass Free

    • Renewals

    • No Shows

  • Find an Apple Pay transaction with one of the old ChargeBys for Apple Pay → Make sure you can refund that after the data cleanup script is run

    • Work with Yoel to set up test data

    • Do a no show with and old ChargeBy as well

comments.author: Yoel Martin Pardo
comments.body: QA: We will need to test refunds with previous Subscriptions, reservations and noshows. Also we will need to do regression on everything again to make sure that everything is working properly. Previous records using chargeBy ApplePay, they should be using GP processors now.
comments.bodyHtml: QA: We will need to test refunds with previous Subscriptions, reservations and noshows. Also we will need to do regression on everything again to make sure that everything is working properly. Previous records using chargeBy ApplePay, they should be using GP processors now.

issues.summary: Modernize Affiliate API dependencies to remediate security vulnerabilities
issues.description:
Modernize the Affiliate API's application dependencies and supporting configuration to remediate identified security vulnerabilities and improve compatibility with .NET Framework 4.8.

Changes

- Updated and revalidated application dependencies to ensure compatibility with .NET Framework 4.8.

- Corrected package, assembly, and analyzer references impacted by dependency upgrades.

- Modernized and consolidated dependency injection configuration used throughout the application.

- Added and corrected service registrations required for application startup and runtime dependency resolution.

- Improved application startup and configuration initialization to align with current dependency versions.

- Removed obsolete configuration artifacts and legacy project references that were no longer required.

- Reviewed and updated client-side dependency configuration and JavaScript resources.

- Evaluated Bootstrap and front-end dependencies while preserving compatibility with the existing Bootstrap 3 user interface.

- Investigated reported security vulnerabilities, including a Handlebars vulnerability, and verified that Handlebars is not directly referenced or loaded by the application.

- Improved maintainability, readability, and consistency of application configuration and dependency management.
issues.descriptionHtml:
Modernize the Affiliate API's application dependencies and supporting configuration to remediate identified security vulnerabilities and improve compatibility with .NET Framework 4.8.

Changes

  • Updated and revalidated application dependencies to ensure compatibility with .NET Framework 4.8.

  • Corrected package, assembly, and analyzer references impacted by dependency upgrades.

  • Modernized and consolidated dependency injection configuration used throughout the application.

  • Added and corrected service registrations required for application startup and runtime dependency resolution.

  • Improved application startup and configuration initialization to align with current dependency versions.

  • Removed obsolete configuration artifacts and legacy project references that were no longer required.

  • Reviewed and updated client-side dependency configuration and JavaScript resources.

  • Evaluated Bootstrap and front-end dependencies while preserving compatibility with the existing Bootstrap 3 user interface.

  • Investigated reported security vulnerabilities, including a Handlebars vulnerability, and verified that Handlebars is not directly referenced or loaded by the application.

  • Improved maintainability, readability, and consistency of application configuration and dependency management.

comments.author: Tyler Olechnowicz
comments.body:
QA Testing Instructions

- Verify the Affiliate API application loads successfully.

- Sign in with a valid account and verify authentication functions as expected.

- Perform a full regression test of the application and verify pages load and function as expected.

- Verify application navigation and menus render correctly.

- Verify browser console is free of JavaScript errors during testing.

- Confirm no regressions are observed as a result of the dependency and configuration updates.

Expected Result:
The application loads successfully, authentication works as expected, pages render and function correctly, and no regressions are introduced by the dependency and configuration updates.
comments.bodyHtml:
QA Testing Instructions

  1. Verify the Affiliate API application loads successfully.

  2. Sign in with a valid account and verify authentication functions as expected.

  3. Perform a full regression test of the application and verify pages load and function as expected.

  4. Verify application navigation and menus render correctly.

  5. Verify browser console is free of JavaScript errors during testing.

  6. Confirm no regressions are observed as a result of the dependency and configuration updates.

Expected Result:
The application loads successfully, authentication works as expected, pages render and function correctly, and no regressions are introduced by the dependency and configuration updates.

issues.description:
On the Tee Time Reservation email sent to golfers, there are two different bodies that reference benefits of GolfPass. One as a member and one as a non-member. We need to update the text in these versions. See attached for the two versions.

GolfPass Member - Anyone with ProductID [36, 37, 42, 62, 69, 71] - Attached image Screenshot 2026-03-02 at 3.23.43 PM.png

- Can we confirm what validation we are using to determine what member they are? We got a report that some members are receiving the non member version. Confirm with Brian when you find the logic - Luis Confirmed the logic is based on ProductId

- Change “Extra GolfPass Rewards Points” to: Earn or redeem GolfPass Points on tee time bookings

Non-Member OR anyone with a ProductId that does not match the above list- Attached image image (24).png

- Change “Earn and redeem GolfPass Points…” to: Earn GolfPass Points on tee time bookings and redeem for a discount on GolfPass+ membership

- Remove $40 Taylormade credit

- Change the Peacock benefit to: Complimentary access to Peacock Premium (up to 12 months)
issues.descriptionHtml:
On the Tee Time Reservation email sent to golfers, there are two different bodies that reference benefits of GolfPass. One as a member and one as a non-member. We need to update the text in these versions. See attached for the two versions.

GolfPass Member - Anyone with ProductID [36, 37, 42, 62, 69, 71] - Attached image Screenshot 2026-03-02 at 3.23.43 PM.png

  • Can we confirm what validation we are using to determine what member they are? We got a report that some members are receiving the non member version. Confirm with Brian when you find the logic - Luis Confirmed the logic is based on ProductId

  • Change “Extra GolfPass Rewards Points” to: Earn or redeem GolfPass Points on tee time bookings

Non-Member OR anyone with a ProductId that does not match the above list- Attached image image (24).png

  • Change “Earn and redeem GolfPass Points…” to: Earn GolfPass Points on tee time bookings and redeem for a discount on GolfPass+ membership

  • Remove $40 Taylormade credit

  • Change the Peacock benefit to: Complimentary access to Peacock Premium (up to 12 months)

comments.author: Luis Vallejo Mursuli
comments.body:
QA,

Here are the instructions to test this ticket. Contact me if you have questions.

Thanks!

Here are the instructions to test this ticket. Contact me if you have questions.

testCases.title: GolfPass+ Member Confirmation Email - ProductId 36
testCases.description: Test ID: TC-CORE14233-A1 Priority: High Test Type: End-to-End Email Validation
checks: Member benefits header is displayed
All 4 benefit bullet points are present
"Earn or redeem GolfPass Points on tee time bookings" text is correct (NOT "Extra GolfPass Rewards Points")
Phone number 833-465-3847 is displayed twice
Support contact text is displayed at the end of the section
No non-member benefits text is displayed

testCases.title: GolfPass+ Member Confirmation Email - ProductId Variation
testCases.description: Test ID: TC-CORE14233-A2 Priority: High Test Type: End-to-End Email Validation
checks: Email received successfully
Member benefits section displays correctly
All 4 benefit bullet points are present and correct
"Earn or redeem GolfPass Points on tee time bookings" text is displayed
No non-member benefits section visible

testCases.title: Non-Member Confirmation Email - Standard Non-Member
testCases.description: Test ID: TC-CORE14233-B1 Priority: High Test Type: End-to-End Email Validation
checks: Non-member header is displayed
Non-member benefits description is present
All 5 benefit bullet points are present
"Earn GolfPass Points on tee time bookings and redeem for a discount on GolfPass+ membership" text is correct (NOT "Earn and redeem GolfPass Points…")
Peacock benefit states "Complimentary access to Peacock Premium (up to 12 months)"
No "$40 TaylorMade credit" is mentioned anywhere
Call-to-action link to golfpass.com is present and clickable
No member benefits section is visible

testCases.title: Non-Member Confirmation Email - Verify Removed Content
testCases.description: Test ID: TC-CORE14233-B2 Priority: High Test Type: Regression Test - Removed Content Validation
checks: Search email for "TaylorMade" - should return 0 results
Search email for "$40" - should return 0 results
Search email for "Extra GolfPass Rewards Points" - should return 0 results
Search email for "NBCUniversal" - should return 0 results
Confirm new Peacock text includes "Peacock Premium (up to 12 months)"

testCases.title: ProductId Boundary Validation - ProductId 35 (Non-Member)
testCases.description: Test ID: TC-CORE14233-C1 Priority: Medium Test Type: Boundary Condition Test
checks: Non-member header "Looking to get even more out of your game?" is displayed
Non-member benefits list (5 items) is present
Member benefits section is NOT visible
ProductId 35 correctly triggers non-member template (not member template)

testCases.title: ProductTypeId Requirement Validation
testCases.description: Test ID: TC-CORE14233-C2 Priority: Medium Test Type: Conditional Logic Test
checks: Email received successfully
GolfPass Value Prop section is NOT present
No member benefits section visible
No non-member benefits section visible
Standard reservation details are displayed correctly
ProductTypeId = 9 requirement is properly enforced
Development environment is accessible (https://dev.golfnow.com)
Test customer accounts are configured with correct ProductIds
Email inboxes are accessible for all test accounts
Select 2-3 test facilities with available tee times
Record facility names and available time slots
Verify pricing is configured correctly
Print or have digital copy of test cases ready
Email client open for quick verification
Screenshot tool ready for capturing email examples
Header: "As a GolfPass+ member, every tee time you book includes exclusive benefits:"
